findings.evidence: grep: SquareClass.lean:143 `theorem squareClass_of_sq_mem`, plus _fin/_fintype/_sqrt_mem; SquareClassIndependence.lean:54 `def squareClass`, both in `namespace TauCeti.Multiquadratic`.
findings.fix: Namespace the def, e.g. `Units.squareClass`, so its lemmas become `Units.squareClass_eq_zero_iff` / `Units.squareClass_prod`, distinct from the descent family.
findings.issue: The new `def squareClass` claims the `squareClass_` prefix, but `TauCeti.Multiquadratic` already has `squareClass_of_sq_mem`, `_fin`, `_fintype`, `_sqrt_mem` (SquareClass.lean) using `squareClass` as a topic word for square-class descent. A reader can no longer tell which `squareClass_*` are projections of this def (`squareClass_eq_zero_iff`, `squareClass_prod`) versus the unrelated descent theorems.

findings.evidence: Line 129 `theorem isSquare_units_val_iff {u : Kˣ} : IsSquare (u : K) ↔ IsSquare u`; no `isSquare_val`/`isSquare_coe` precedent found in ./mathlib.
findings.issue: `isSquare_units_val_iff` names the units coercion in the middle of the lemma rather than via namespace; convention for `IsSquare ↑u ↔ IsSquare u` is to namespace the coercion.

summary: Names mostly describe their conclusions in standard terminology, but the new def `squareClass` collides with the existing `squareClass_of_*` descent-theorem family in the same namespace, making the `squareClass_` prefix ambiguous. One coercion lemma is named off-convention.
